note: AS BUILT v0.6.6. A new gated, elbow-only post-route pass avoidSubgraphs() runs FIRST in finishEdges (before offsetLabelsOffLine/separateLanes). For an edge whose long axis-aligned interior trunk pierces a container box that does NOT hold BOTH endpoints, it pushes the trunk just outside the container's nearest side + MARGIN(28) via the existing moveLane, then lowerReentry() drops the re-entry corner to a short APPROACH(30) near the interior endpoint (using the edge's own border anchor — computePerimeterPorts UNTOUCHED, D2=A). The gate: run.along strictly inside the container cross-span AND parallel overlap >= MIN_CROSS(120); both-endpoints-inside skipped; an approach-into-a-member run (i==1 & from in c, or i==len-3 & to in c) skipped to keep the pass idempotent. computeAvoidContainers() builds the obstacle set from computeSubgraphBoxes + resolveMemberNodes; threaded through finishEdges' new optional 5th param + layout()/applyPositions(); native/state+class pass no containers (no-op). Mirrored byte-for-byte in the vnmRuntime twin (avoidSubgraphs + avoidContainersFrom), called first in BOTH renderEdges (subgraphWorldBox) and buildSvg (subgraphAbsBox). Proven to fire ONLY on architecture.mmd's BE<->RULES across the whole corpus; both subgraph heroes + all snapshots byte-identical. Version 0.6.5->0.6.6.

diagrams.title: As built: avoidSubgraphs() runs first in finishEdges, reads container obstacles from computeAvoidContainers, mirrored byte-for-byte in the vnmRuntime twin; native/state passes no containers
As built: avoidSubgraphs() per-edge/per-run/per-container decision flow — both-in + approach-into-member skips, MIN_CROSS(120) gate, push nearest-side +/-MARGIN(28), lowerReentry APPROACH(30)
